t_ch: 开玩笑吧？这是…爻光将军的病历？
t_en: No way... Are these... General Yao Guang's medical records?

t_ch: 你随手翻动电脑桌面的文档，尚未归档的往期病历简直人才辈出。
t_en: You casually browse through the documents on your computer desktop, finding those unarchived medical records are a real parade of characters.

t_ch: 一位患者自称是太一本尊，给她点赞的人都是受赐福的「秩序」行者。
t_en: A patient claims to be Ena incarnate, and those who give them a thumbs up are blessed pathstriders of the Order.

t_ch: 下一页记录更为荒诞，患者宣称他才是太一，那个自称是太一的病人，其实是疯掉的药师。
t_en: The next record is even more absurd. The patient claims that HE is Ena, and that the person who claims to be Ena is actually Yaoshi who has gone mad.

t_ch: 下一页，患者四处寻找「空格键」，他认为自己所在的系统没有跳跃功能。再下一页……
t_en: Next page, the patient searches everywhere for the "Space Bar," believing that their system lacks a jump function. Next page...

t_ch: 你下意识地移开目光，有的事情，还是不要知道的太多为好。
t_en: You instinctively look away. Some things are better left unknown.

t_ch: 病历就放在书架上，左手第一个文件。
t_en: The medical records are on the bookshelf, first file on the left.

t_ch: 城市里已经开始出现受害者了吗？
t_en: Are there already victims in the city?

t_ch: 要想清除模因病毒，帮助零号病人认清现实可是治疗的重要一环。
t_en: To eliminate the memetic virus, helping Patient Zero face reality is a crucial phase of the treatment.

t_ch: 怎么回事？
t_en: What's happening?

t_ch: 你从没来过「致命玩笑」吗？
t_en: You've never been to Fatal Joke before?

t_ch: 这不奇怪，我在「酒馆」呆着的时间不多。除此之外，我必须重申，我很健康。
t_en: That's not surprising. I don't spend much time at the tavern. And to reiterate: I'm perfectly healthy.

t_ch: 哈哈，我喜欢讳疾忌医的病人，我会亲手击碎他们的自信。
t_en: Haha, I love patients who deny their illness. I'll personally shatter their self-confidence.

t_ch: 花火小姐，接下来我要为您开具一份详细的诊断报告！就趁这工夫，请您和同病相怜的病友谈谈吧。
t_en: Miss Sparkle, I'm going to write up a detailed diagnosis report for you! In the meantime, why don't you chat with your fellow patients who share your affliction?

t_ch: 花火小姐，在诊断结束前，还请您在这个房间稍等片刻。
t_en: Miss Sparkle, please wait in this room until the consultation is complete.

t_ch: 你好啊，你也和我被同样的疾病困扰吗？
t_en: Hey there! Are you also troubled by the same illness as me?

t_ch: 你得了什么病？
t_en: What illness do you have?

t_ch: 我曾经患上过一种名叫「愚者」的病。
t_en: I once suffered from a disease called "Fool."

t_ch: 愚者是什么病？
t_en: What kind of disease is "Fool"?

t_ch: 我的身上长出了一个名为「花火」的假面愚者，她慢慢和我融为了一体。她用我来承担扮演时所有的痛苦，她让我遍体鳞伤，自己却狡猾地躲过了一切……
t_en: A Masked Fool named "Sparkle" grew out of me, slowly becoming one with me. She used me to bear all the pain caused by her performances, left me battered and bruised, while she slipped away from everything...

t_ch: 不过我的运气够好，我找到了这个名叫「花火」的病灶，我把她…把这颗万恶的毒瘤排出了体外。
t_en: But I was lucky enough to find this tumor called "Sparkle," and I... expelled this malignant growth from my body.

t_ch: 你只是个被我抛下的道具而已！
t_en: You're just a tool I discarded!

t_ch: 花火，你还认得我吗？
t_en: Sparkle, do you still remember me?

t_ch: 今天叫对我名字的人可比过去一周多多了！好感动！
t_en: More people got my name right today than in the past week! I'm so touched!

t_ch: 我是你最初的面具啊…「花火型角色」面具。
t_en: I am your original mask... the "Sparkle Role" mask.

t_ch: 花火型角色？
t_en: The Sparkle role?

t_ch: 就是那种生命短暂而璀璨的少女类型角色：和仇敌同归于尽的复仇者、殉情的痴情儿、陷入狂乱的年轻艺术家……
t_en: The archetype of a bright maiden doomed for a short life: the avenger who perishes with her nemesis, the lover who dies for love, the young artist driven to madness...

t_ch: 你用我赢得了名声、掌声和数不尽的热切目光——可为什么却要残忍地抛下我呢？
t_en: You used me to win fame, applause, and countless eager gazes... so why would you cruelly cast me aside?

t_ch: 求求你，再佩戴我一次吧……
t_en: Please, wear me once more...

t_ch: 一个演员最不该做的，就是让自己的存在感被捆绑在某一类角色上呢~
t_en: The worst thing an actor should do is be tied down to one type of role.

t_ch: 你还是给我安心躺好吧！
t_en: Do yourself a favor and stay down!

t_ch: 嘻嘻，花火，你回来啦！
t_en: Hehe, Sparkle, you're back!

t_ch: 你看起来很高兴嘛。
t_en: You seem happy.

t_ch: 它真的高兴吗？
t_en: Is it really happy?

t_ch: 是、是啊。我一直按着她的要求，保持情绪高昂呢~
t_en: Y—Yeah. I'm keeping my spirits high, just like she asked!

t_ch: 花火花火，是你告诉我，爱笑的面具运气不会太差，不是吗？
t_en: Sparkle, Sparkle, you're the one who told me that a smiling mask will never have bad luck, aren't you?

t_ch: 这个世界不想看到太多悲伤的、残忍的、愤怒的、痛苦的情绪…是你告诉我，任何时候只要微笑就好。
t_en: This world doesn't want to see too much sadness, cruelty, rage, or pain... You told me that I just need to smile, no matter what.

t_ch: 我明明都做到了…但你，为什么要抛下我呢？我现在……我现在只想大吼！大哭！大叫！
t_en: I did everything you asked... but why did you abandon me? Right now... Right now, I just want to scream! To cry! To shout!

t_ch: <size=36>我只想——把你大口吞下！！！</size>
t_en: <size=36>I just want to... DEVOUR YOU WHOLE!!!</size>

t_ch: 你到底抛弃了多少面具啊？
t_en: Just how many masks have you thrown away?

t_ch: 别在意啦！每个人一生都要抛下同样多的自我……
t_en: Don't worry about it! Everyone sheds just as many versions of themselves in a lifetime...

t_ch: 不，我不要再长出「花火」来——
t_en: No, I don't want "Sparkle" to grow out of me again...

t_ch: 拜托，把我和那些面具统统戴上吧，就算你没有那么多脸——
t_en: Please... put on all the masks, even if you don't have that many faces...

t_ch: 来啊——让我吃了你！吞下你！
t_en: Come on, let me eat you! I'll swallow you whole!

t_ch: 花火小姐，诊断有结论了。您的主要成分是疯狂、幽默感和表演型人格。很不幸，您的配比失衡，早已病入膏肓了。
t_en: Miss Sparkle, I have your results. Your main components are madness, humor, and a theatrical personality. Unfortunately, the proportions are badly imbalanced. You're beyond saving.

t_ch: 但，不幸中的万幸是，你的面具从你身上脱落了！她自由了！
t_en: But the silver lining is, your mask has fallen off! She's free at last!

t_ch: 这就是一切大家不幸的开始啊。我抛下的每一张面具里还从没有哪一个主意和脾气都这么大的。
t_en: And that's where everyone's misfortune began. None of the masks I've cast off ever had such strong opinions or such a temper.

t_ch: 她不仅仅是你的虚拟形象…
t_en: She's not just your virtual persona...

t_ch: 火花也是你的愚者面具？
t_en: Is Sparxie also one of your Fool's Masks?

t_ch: 她不用再受你的理性之癌侵蚀，她可以做任何你想做而不敢做的事情。
t_en: She no longer needs to be corroded by your cancer of rationality. Now, she can do anything that you wanted to do but didn't dare to do.

t_ch: 笑话，世界上还有什么花火大人不敢做的事情？
t_en: Ha! As if there's anything Miss Sparkle wouldn't dare to do!

t_ch: 举例来说…不敢佩戴一张面具太久。
t_en: For example... you don't dare wear any one mask for too long.

t_ch: 你像蝴蝶蜕去旧皮那样不断抛弃着每一张面具，只为了不被它们定义、束缚，你害怕停下来，停留在某个刻板印象里，你害怕自己有一天不够「欢愉」——
t_en: Like a butterfly shedding old skin, you keep discarding every mask, just so you won't be defined or bound by them. You're afraid to stop, to be stuck in some stereotype. You're afraid that one day you won't be "elating" enough...

t_ch: ——所以你才画出了「火花」小姐，在网络上成为她。
t_en: ...That's why you drew Miss "Sparxie," and became her online.

t_ch: 作为一张光鲜亮丽的面具，人人都会向她倾诉，人人都想成为她，人人都为她贡献出自己的有趣点子，你本以为这会给你的「欢愉」注入生命力——
t_en: As a dazzling mask, everyone confides in her, everyone wants to be her, everyone feeds her their clever ideas. You thought this would inject new life into your "elation"...

t_ch: 但很可惜，<color=#dbc291ff>一旦戴上了面具，人就成了面具的附属，人们只在乎面具描绘的形象，不会有人关心底下的你。</color>
t_en: But sadly, <color=#dbc291ff>once the mask is on, you become its accessory. People care only about the image the mask paints. No one cares about the you beneath.</color>

t_ch: 而这，就是她拥有生命的原因！
t_en: And that is why she came to life!

t_ch: 人类的认同和渴望，会顺着网络的根系为她提供绽放的养料：她会成为一朵妖花，顺应所有人的愿望让人佩戴自己，复制自己，开遍整个二相乐园！多美丽的景象啊……
t_en: Human recognition and desire will flow through the internet, providing nourishment for her to bloom: She'll become a bewitching flower, conforming to everyone's wishes, letting them wear her, become her, and blossom across all of Planarcadia! What a beautiful sight...

t_ch: 医生，你有阻止她的办法吗？
t_en: Doctor, do you have a way to stop her?

t_ch: 「阻止」？你是说…「治疗」对吧？
t_en: "Stop"? You mean... "treat," yes?

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>「火化花火」，这就是我能开出的最佳治疗方案。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Cremate Sparkle. That's the best treatment I can prescribe.</color>

t_ch: 噗，我还以为你能有什么高招呢？
t_en: Pfft. And here I thought you had something clever.

t_ch: 毕竟，一切痛苦有且只有一种彻底的疗法：让自己消失，对吧？
t_en: After all, there's only one radical cure for all suffering: make oneself disappear, right?

t_ch: 花火小姐。可不要觉得我是在对你开玩笑。我只在「酒馆」以外的地方才会没个正形。
t_en: Miss Sparkle, don't think I'm joking around with you. I only misbehave outside the tavern.

t_ch: 而在这里，我是个医生，不带引号的那种，所以我的疗法也真实有效——一般来说，愚者消失时，她的面具也会失去力量成为死物，毕竟你们是互为表里的存在。
t_en: But here I am a doctor, without quotation marks. My treatment is real and effective. Generally, when a Fool vanishes, their mask loses its power and becomes a dead thing. You're two sides of the same coin, after all.

t_ch: 不过，关于「火花」…我说不好，因为在「火花大会」上要佩戴她的，将是二相乐园的亿万普通人，这些人会为她提供滋养和力量。所以，你们的时机稍纵即逝——
t_en: But as for "Sparxie"... I can't say for sure. During SparxiCon, she'll be worn by millions of ordinary people in Planarcadia, feeding her with sustenance and strength. Your window of opportunity is therefore fleeting...

t_ch: 去吧，躺到那张床上去，和你的朋友们告别吧。按下那颗按钮，「护士」会过来把你推走。
t_en: Go on, lie down on that bed and say goodbye to your friends. Press that button, and the "nurse" will come wheel you away.

t_ch: 酒馆里有扇门，确定、一定以及肯定能通往赛杜尼拉默一颗还没熄灭的恒星。「护士」会把你推到那儿去，一了百了。
t_en: There's a door in the tavern that absolutely, positively, leads to a still-burning star in Sedounirama. The "nurse" will send you there, and that'll be the end of it.

t_ch: 我要是说不呢？
t_en: And if I say no?

t_ch: 那就恕我…不客气啦。
t_en: Then I'll have to... be impolite.

t_ch: 花火小姐，{NICKNAME}，选择已经交给你们了。至于我…乐子神在上，我得赶快搭下一班飞船离开这破地方了。
t_en: Miss Sparkle, {NICKNAME}, the choice is yours. As for me... By The Laughter above, I've got to catch the next ship out of this dump.

t_ch: 假面愚者的面具，代表着你们想要成为的样子。你们用它来聚焦自己的力量，但同样面具也会渐渐束缚住你们。
t_en: The mask of the Masked Fool represents what you wish to become. You use it to focus your power, but the mask will gradually bind you as well.

t_ch: 这就是你一直在逃避火花的原因。她从你这边偷走的不只是一筒模因病毒…还有你的力量。
t_en: That's why you've been avoiding Sparxie all this time. She didn't just steal a vial of memetic virus... She stole your strength.

t_ch: 也难怪，吧台的愚者说了这样的笑话：「假面愚者」失去了假面，剩下的不过是个愚者而已。
t_en: No wonder the Fool at the bar joked: when a Masked Fool loses their mask, what remains is just a fool.

t_ch: 是啊，每个愚者都拥有这样一张假面，至少一张。它们不是装饰，而是我们命运的一部分，是我们亲手造就了它们——
t_en: Yes, every Fool has at least one such mask. Not mere decoration, but a part of our destiny, crafted by our own hands...

t_ch: 从悲悼伶人那儿偷走最顺眼的假面；灵感降临时自己亲手绘制的头饰；又或者某一天从天而降扣在自己脸上的东西……
t_en: It might be a Mourning Actor mask you took a liking to and stole from them. It might be a head accessory you personally sketched in a moment of inspiration. Or it might be something that one day fell from the sky and latched onto your face...

t_ch: 能成为愚者的人，至少会经历一次这样的命运时刻：覆面的时刻。
t_en: To become a Fool, one must experience at least one such instance of destiny: the moment of masking one's face.

t_ch: 透过面具的眼洞，我们获得了不同的视角，瞧见了宇宙中翻涌着无穷无尽的玩笑，还有我们作为生命的可笑本质。
t_en: Through the mask's eyeholes, we gain a different perspective, finally seeing how the universe is simply churning with endless jokes and the absurd nature of our existence as living beings.

t_ch: 面具就是我们想成为的样子。如今，这个冒牌货比我更像我了。
t_en: The mask is what we want to be. And now, this impostor is more "me" than I am.

t_ch: 动手吧！按下按钮叫来护士，把花火大人火化了！
t_en: Do it! Press the button, call the nurse, and cremate Miss Sparkle!

t_ch: 至少这样一来，那个冒牌货短时间内会失去力量。你们应该可以轻松打败她的，对吧？
t_en: At least then the impostor will lose her power for a little while. You should be able to take her down easily, right?

t_ch: 呜，你们…不会按铃的，对吧？
t_en: Uh... you're not actually going to press it, right?

t_ch: 给一点时间，让我们犹豫犹豫、考虑考虑。
t_en: Give us a moment... to hesitate, to think.

t_ch: 等待可是很折磨人的。你俩不会是故意要这么惩罚我吧？喂，小灰毛？你好歹说句话呀！
t_en: Waiting is torture, you know. You're not doing this on purpose to punish me, are you? Hey, Gray Hair! Say something!

t_ch: （不按按钮）
t_en: (Don't press the button)

t_ch: 哈哈哈，我就知道！小灰毛，你还是那个有温度的小灰毛！
t_en: Hahaha, I knew it! Gray Hair, you're still the same warm-hearted Gray Hair I know!

t_ch: 悄悄告诉你个事，我刚才趁着大家不注意已经抠掉了按钮的电池。这是个考验，你通过了！
t_en: Psst, let me tell you something. While no one was looking, I already popped the battery out of the button. This was a test, and you passed!

t_ch: （按下按钮）
t_en: (Press the button)

t_ch: 难以置信！小灰毛，你的心比贝洛伯格的外宇宙之冰还冷吗？！
t_en: Unbelievable! Gray Hair, is your heart colder than Belobog's Ice Out of Space!?

t_ch: 嘿嘿，我一早趁着大家不注意抠掉了按钮的电池。这是个考验，你没通过！
t_en: Hehe, I took the battery out of the button earlier while no one watched. It was a test, and you failed!

t_ch: （狂按按钮！）
t_en: (Frantically mashing buttons!)

t_ch: 喂喂喂喂！小灰毛，咱们的交情你都忘光了吗？按钮都快被按冒烟啦！
t_en: Hey hey hey! Gray Hair, have you forgotten our friendship? You'll make the button smoke at this rate!

t_ch: 可惜，我一早趁着大家不注意抠掉了按钮的电池。这是个考验，你没通过！
t_en: Too bad. I took the battery out of the button earlier while no one watched. It was a test, and you failed!

t_ch: 既然「愚者」自己做出了选择，她应该也想好了回收「假面」的办法，对吧？
t_en: Since the Fool made her choice, she must have a plan to reclaim the Mask, right?

t_ch: 如果你敢说个「不」字，我可要按下按钮，让天基炮来「火化」你咯？你那不干净的小手应该没能快到偷走这块电池吧？
t_en: If you dare say "no," I'll press THIS button and let the orbital cannon "cremate" you for real. Your sticky little fingers aren't fast enough to steal this battery, are they?

t_ch: 有办法有办法！办法总是有的！
t_en: There's a way! There's always a way!

t_ch: 花火大人一定会负起责任，和大家齐心协力阻止「火花大会」的！
t_en: Miss Sparkle will definitely take responsibility and work together with everyone to stop SparxiCon!

t_ch: 这掌声又是怎么回事？
t_en: What's with this applause?

t_ch: 我们…是不是一直都在被谁看着？
t_en: Have we... been watched the whole time?

t_ch: 所以，咱们刚才治疗的一举一动都被观众们看在眼里？…这到底是医院还是戏院？
t_en: So the audience watched our every move in that "treatment"? ...Is this a hospital or a theater?

t_ch: 这是人生，生命不息，评判不止。在你看不到的角落里，总有数不尽的评判的目光，为你诊断，为你开出「药方」。
t_en: That's life for you. While there's life, there's judgment. In every unseen corner, countless judging eyes are watching, diagnosing, and writing their own "prescriptions."

t_ch: 我不得不承认，「致命玩笑」这地方实在有趣。没能早些到这里逛逛，实在是可惜了。
t_en: I must admit, Fatal Joke is a fascinating place. Real shame I didn't come check it out earlier.

t_ch: 别管这些观众了，他们只是来找乐子的，不会帮忙，也帮不上忙。
t_en: Ignore the spectators. They're just here for laughs. They won't help, and couldn't if they tried.

t_ch: 你刚才说到你有办法阻止火花，说说吧，你打算怎么做？
t_en: You said you had a way to stop Sparxie. Let's hear it. What's the plan?

t_ch: 我收藏着几件…「反花火武装」。
t_en: I've got a few... "Anti-Sparkle Weapons" in my collection.

t_ch: 那是什么？
t_en: What's that?

t_ch: 看名字就知道了吧？是花火大人在过去的冒险中收集的道具，也是花火大人的劲敌曾经用来对付我的武器。
t_en: The name says it all. These are items Miss Sparkle collected in my past adventures, and weapons that my fierce rivals once used against me.

t_ch: 为什么要反自己？
t_en: Why would you go against yourself?

t_ch: 我一直有预感，有一天花火大人会变成这个宇宙中谁也无法遏制的恐怖存在。在那一天到来前，必须有人想办法阻止我才行啊！
t_en: I've always had this premonition that one day Miss Sparkle would become an unstoppable terror in this universe. Someone has to find a way to stop me before that day comes!

t_ch: 一次旅行，我遇见了某个和我志趣相投，颇有愚者天分的小姑娘。
t_en: On a trip, I met a girl who shared my tastes. She had quite the knack to be a Fool.

t_ch: 在和她斗智斗勇的过程中，她用当地特有的「风滚草」差点把我砸成了花火酱。她还有一台巨大的机甲，身上每个开口都能冲我发射光束。
t_en: In our battle of wits, she nearly turned me into Sparkle paste using the local "Tumbleweed" specialty. She also had a massive mech with beam cannons up the wazoo, ready to blast away any time.

t_ch: 我把这些旅途中遇到的厉害宝贝全都悄悄复刻了一份。有了这些「反花火武装」，一定能叫这个冒牌货吃不了兜着走!
t_en: I quietly replicated those powerful goodies from my travels. With these Anti-Sparkle Weapons, that impostor won't know what hit her!

t_ch: 不得不说，我真是有先见之明！
t_en: I gotta say, I'm quite the visionary!

t_ch: 两位，接下来咱们走寄存区。我已经联系好了乔瓦尼，他正在那儿清点失物呢。
t_en: You two, let's head to the storage area next. I've already contacted Giovanni. He's doing an inventory check of the lost items there.

t_ch: 是学校那边传来了情报？
t_en: Intel from the school?

t_ch: 潜伏记者没找到人质。
t_en: The undercover journalist didn't find the hostages.

t_ch: 潜伏记者发现了特别情况。
t_en: The undercover journalist discovered something unusual.

t_ch: 狸猫记者们没找到三月和杨叔，倒是发现火花正在拆除学校中庭的海浪景画。
t_en: The furbo journalist couldn't find March or Mr. Yang, but they did spot Sparxie taking down the wave mural in the school courtyard.

t_ch: 喔？令人在意的行为。看来火花那边也在紧锣密鼓地准备着，但她为什么要做这些？
t_en: Oh? That's noteworthy. Looks like Sparxie's busy making preparations too. But why do all this?

t_ch: 先不管她，去愚者们的寄存区看看吧。
t_en: Never mind her for now. Let's check out the Fools' storage area.

t_ch: 哟，老乔，你好啊。你在那上面干什么？
t_en: Hey, Gio. What are you doing up there?

t_ch: 当然是在为你奔忙，为你清点损失啊！等等，你把谁带来了？
t_en: Working my tail off for you, tallying the losses! Wait, who'd you bring with you?

t_ch: 是{NICKNAME}啊！我就知道你总有一天会踏入「酒馆」成为我们的一员的。毕竟，拥有假面愚者潜质的人是会相互吸引的。
t_en: Oh, it's {NICKNAME}! I knew you'd step into the tavern and become one of us someday. Those with the makings of Masked Fools are naturally drawn to one another.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你认识的愚者朋友可真不少。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you sure know quite a few Fools.

t_ch: 您身后这位美丽的女士…喔，想必是列车领航员姬子女士了。初次见面，请多关照。
t_en: And the lovely lady behind you... you must be Miss Himeko, Navigator of the Express. A pleasure to meet you.

t_ch: 乔瓦尼先生，叫我姬子就好。
t_en: Mr. Giovanni, just call me Himeko.

t_ch: 本来，非愚者不能踏入此处，但既然是花火将你们领来，{NICKNAME}也算老朋友了，我作为保管员可以破个例。让我为各位介绍一下这里——
t_en: Normally only Fools may enter, but since Sparkle brought you, and {NICKNAME} is practically an old friend, I can make an exception as the caretaker. Let me show you around...

t_ch: 老乔，别啰啰嗦嗦的。桑博说你逢人就讲那句介绍词，他都快听出老茧来了——
t_en: Gio, cut the spiel. Sampo says you recite that intro so often he's grown calluses on his ears...

t_ch: 想要愚者们的财宝、纪念品和恶作剧道具吗？就来寄存区「大秘宝」吧！他们把一切都放在了这里。
t_en: Want the Fools' treasure, souvenirs, and prank gear? Come to the Grand Treasury storage! They've stashed everything here.

t_ch: 你又抢我台词。
t_en: You stole my line again.

t_ch: 这是在招贼吗？
t_en: Are you trying to advertise to thieves or what?

t_ch: 嗯哼，道具失窃率这么高，多半是这句介绍词害的。
t_en: Pfft. With our theft rate, that tagline's probably to blame.

t_ch: 这句话…有些耳熟？
t_en: That line... sounds familiar?

t_ch: 我在某个世界旅行时听来的，你也听过这句话对不对？
t_en: Picked it up on a trip to another world. You've heard this phrase too, haven't you?

t_ch: 必须承认，这句话很有诱惑力，所以我才会拿它来当宣传语了——寄存区的造访率此后提升了<unbreak>26%</unbreak>。
t_en: I'll admit, it's catchy. That's why I used it as a slogan. Storage area visits went up by <unbreak>26%</unbreak> after that.

t_ch: 正事要紧。我刚才发来的提货消息你瞧见了没，我收藏的「反花火武装」呢？
t_en: Business first. Did you see my pickup message I sent? Where's my stored Anti-Sparkle Weapons?

t_ch: 你的寄存清单杂乱无章，从来不做备注。如果不是这次把我叫来检查，天知道你到底藏了多少惊喜？至于「反花火武装」…我倒想问问，那又是什么东西？
t_en: Your storage list is a total mess. You never leave any notes! If I hadn't been called to audit, who'd know how many surprises you got stashed away? As for the "Anti-Sparkle Weapons"... I'd love to know what on earth that's supposed to be?

t_ch: 唉，你还是自己进去找找吧！
t_en: Bah, why don't you go in and look for yourself!

t_ch: 按你的要求，我把金库入口接通了你的收藏室。
t_en: As requested, I've linked the vault entrance to your collection room.

t_ch: 星琼，关于这类宝石的来历众说纷纭。有人说那是太初宇宙成形后冷却的光。有人说它是逝去古兽的灵髓，历亿万年时间凝结如石。
t_en: Stellar Jade... There are many theories about where these gems came from. Some say they're light that cooled and crystallized after the primordial universe took shape. Others claim they're the crystallized essence of ancient Leviathans, formed over billions of years.

t_ch: 毫无疑问，它颠倒众生的美丽足可换取这宇宙间的一切——物质的享受，忆庭的光锥，古老的遗器，还有他人的追随……
t_en: Without a doubt, its mesmerizing beauty could be traded for everything in this universe — material pleasures, Light Cones of the Garden, ancient Relics, and the devotion of others...

t_ch: 眼前有这么大一颗，它足以令所有人为之疯狂——没错，你要它！你很想要它！你想得快要发狂啦——
t_en: There's such a huge one right in front of you, big enough to drive everyone crazy... That's right, you want it! You really want it! You're about to go into a frenzy—

t_ch: 我的…宝贝。
t_en: My precioussss...

t_ch: 它在对我说话？
t_en: Is it talking to me?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，醒醒！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, wake up!
...Huh?

t_ch: 喜欢这件意外的礼物吗？哈哈，星琼确实是诱捕无名客最好的陷阱。
t_en: Enjoy the surprise gift? Haha, Stellar Jade is the perfect bait for catching the Nameless.

t_ch: 你还挺好心，我第一次见到贼偷了东西竟然还会把更值钱的宝贝放进金库里。
t_en: How generous of you. First time I've seen a thief steal something and leave something even more valuable in the vault.

t_ch: 这不正是我们假面愚者最喜欢的怪盗格调吗？拿走了精心收纳的藏品，就得奉上一件礼物作为回报，安抚失主的心。
t_en: Isn't this exactly the type of phantom-thief flair we Masked Fools adore? Remove a carefully curated piece, and leave a gift in place to soothe the owner.

t_ch: 噗，一颗超大号的星琼，确实抵得上一罐模因病毒和几件「反花火武装」的价值不是吗？
t_en: Heh, a jumbo-sized Stellar Jade is surely worth a jar of Memetic Virus and a few "Anti-Sparkle Weapons," wouldn't you say?

t_ch: …你还偷走了我收藏的其他宝贝！
t_en: ...And you stole my other prized pieces too!?

t_ch: 那些不值钱的破垃圾？就当是我附赠了一次金库保洁服务吧？
t_en: That worthless junk? Consider it a complimentary vault cleaning.

t_ch: 在「火花大会」开始前，我想先来一点娱兴的小节目。旧型号，还有无名客，你们应该能突破这个房间的吧？可不要让我失望哟。
t_en: Before SparxiCon begins, I'd like a little warm-up entertainment. Old model, and the Nameless, you should be able to break out of this room, right? Don't disappoint me now.

t_ch: 老乔，这里的安保工作真是一塌糊涂！
t_en: Gio, the security here is an absolute mess!

t_ch: 你可没资格这么说。这儿连酒保都是临时工，寄存区能有我这样的好心人帮你点数，你就该偷着笑了。
t_en: You're in no position to talk. Even the drinksmith here is a temp. Count yourself lucky I'm kind enough to help tally your storage.

t_ch: 她不仅偷走了我的力量、我的收藏，还对花火大人的形象造成了毁灭打击！今天…真是最糟糕的一天！
t_en: She didn't just steal my power and my collection... she completely destroyed Miss Sparkle's reputation! This is... the worst day ever!

t_ch: 容我纠正，是花火小姐「迄今为止最糟糕的一天」。
t_en: Correction: Miss Sparkle's "worst day so far."

t_ch: 想嘲笑我吗？尽管来吧。
t_en: Want to make fun of me? Go ahead.

t_ch: 别误会。我也不觉得「齿轮风滚草」和「巨大机甲」能对散布模因病毒的对手奏效。
t_en: Don't get me wrong. I also don't think "gear tumbleweeds" and "giant mechs" would work on someone spreading memetic viruses.

t_ch: 就在刚才，我想到一个法子，也许能压制绘世学院里的模因传染……
t_en: I thought of a way just now that might suppress the memetic contagion at Graphia Academy...

t_ch: 不愧是姬子，还有备用方案？
t_en: As expected of Himeko. You have a backup plan?

t_ch: 火力要用火力压制。而模因病毒，可以被「水」冲刷殆尽。
t_en: You can fight fire with fire, but a memetic virus can be scoured away with "water."

t_ch: 什么意思？
t_en: What do you mean?

t_ch: 我们在学校的探子发来了线报，说火花正在拆除学校中心的景画。
t_en: Our informant at the school reports Sparxie is removing the central mural there.

t_ch: 那幅幻造景画「覆世沧浪图」据说是由绘世本人亲自留下的作品。相传，这朵浪花的来源本是她用来洗去画上错笔的鸽川之水，绘师们说它受到了祝福，因此将它留在学校里。
t_en: The imagenated mural "Worldbreaker Waves" is said to be a work left behind by Graphia herself. Legend says the wave comes from the Dovebrook river-water she used to wash off her painting mistakes. The artists say it is blessed. That's why they kept it in the school.

t_ch: 我原本想不通为什么火花要大费周章地拆除它。现在我明白了——「错笔」是染污画卷的冗余信息，而模因病毒则是染污人类心灵的冗余信息。
t_en: I couldn't understand why Sparxie went to such lengths to tear it down. Now I do. "Painting mistakes" are redundant information that stain a painting. A memetic virus is redundant information that stains the mind.

t_ch: 要是有人懂得操纵这道鸽川之浪，冲刷这座学校，就能洗刷她散布的感染。这就是火花要藏起那座景画的理由。
t_en: If someone knew how to harness this Dovebrook wave to wash over the school, they could cleanse the infection she spread. That's why Sparxie wanted to hide the mural.

t_ch: 所以解决方案就在学校里？
t_en: So the solution lies within the school?

t_ch: 你说的没错。
t_en: Exactly.

t_ch: 也许可以呼叫丹恒使用云吟？
t_en: Maybe we can ask Dan Heng to use cloudhymn?

t_ch: 景画上的那朵浪花并非物理意义的水流，不懂幻造技艺的人无法操控。
t_en: The waves on the painting aren't physical water. Only those versed in the art of imagenesis can control them.

t_ch: 作为绘世学院毕业的学生，我略通绘艺。<color=#dbc291ff>眼下最直接有效的法子，就是突入学校，夺回对那幅景画的控制——用它来压制火花的行动。</color>
t_en: As a Graphia Academy alumna, I know a bit of traditional painting. <color=#dbc291ff>Right now, our most directly effective plan is to break into the school, regain control of the mural, and use it to suppress Sparxie's movements.</color>

t_ch: 走吧，我要让这个冒牌货百倍奉还！
t_en: Let's go! I'll make this impostor pay a hundred-fold!

t_ch: 看来这次相逢注定要行色匆匆，我在此预先祝各位一路顺风。
t_en: Looks like our paths must part in haste. I wish you all fair winds on your journey ahead.

t_ch: 对了，如果各位自觉前途未卜，心中仍有犹豫，我有个小小的建议——请顺道去「极乐之星」的灯球里看看。
t_en: Oh! If any of you feel uncertain about what's next, here's a small suggestion: Take a detour inside the "Blisstar" light orb.

t_ch: 我们可没时间浪费了。
t_en: We don't have time to waste.

t_ch: 出战前去向神明作一次小小祈祷，我想无论如何都不能算是浪费时间吧？
t_en: A quick prayer to the gods before battle hardly counts as wasting time, does it?

t_ch: 那又是什么地方？
t_en: And what is that place?

t_ch: 对我等假面愚者来说，这座灯球的意义就像是空中那轮幻月一般重要。
t_en: For us Masked Fools, that light orb holds the same significance as the Phantasmoon hanging above.

t_ch: 在灯球的深处，每个人都能寻获「欢愉」的神启。
t_en: Deep within the orb, everyone can find the divine revelations of the Elation.

t_ch: 糟了……
t_en: Darn it...

t_ch: 学校那边有别的发现？
t_en: They found something else at the school?

t_ch: 潜伏记者被抓了。
t_en: The undercover journalist got caught.

t_ch: 得抓紧行动的步伐了。
t_en: We need to pick up the pace.

t_ch: 乔瓦尼说，在灯球深处我们能寻到「欢愉」的神启？这座灯球莫非又是一个酒馆房间？
t_en: Giovanni said we could find the divine revelations of the Elation inside the light orb? Don't tell me this orb is another tavern room?

t_ch: Bingo！你的直觉可真准，这东西不是一颗负责制造光污染的灯球。你把它当成愚者们的神龛好了。
t_en: Bingo! Your intuition's spot-on. It isn't just a light-polluting orb. Think of it as a shrine for Fools.

t_ch: 不是说阿哈不准入内吗？
t_en: Wasn't Aha banned from entering?

t_ch: 年轻人只是开革了乐子神进入「酒馆」的资格，可不拒绝与乐子神有关的东西。
t_en: The youngsters only revoked The Laughter's right to enter the tavern. They don't reject things associated with THEM.

t_ch: 崇拜阿哈的神龛？
t_en: A shrine to worship Aha?

t_ch: 噗，说「崇拜」未免有点太认真了不是？
t_en: Pfft, "worship" sounds a bit too serious, no?

t_ch: 假面愚者大部分人都把乐子神当自己看不见也不正经的损友…偶然在心里面找祂碰个杯还行，祂要真找上你那多半没好事。
t_en: Most Masked Fools treat The Laughter like an invisible, mischievous drinking buddy. Fine for a mental toast, but if THEY really come looking for you, it's usually bad news.

t_ch: 就在这樽灯球里，封印着一件与阿哈有关的奇迹。它会回应每个靠近它的愚者。
t_en: Inside this light orb is a miracle tied to Aha. It responds to any Fool who approaches.

t_ch: 我带你们开开眼界。来吧，芝麻开门——
t_en: Let me show you something mind-blowing. Here we go. Open sesame...

t_ch: 我说！芝、麻、开、门！芝麻开门…芝麻？开门？
t_en: I said! O-PEN! SE-SA-ME! Open sesame... Sesame? Open?

t_ch: …不会吧！在祂眼中，我连假面愚者都算不上了？这还真是个「大惊喜」啊，那冒牌货把我的身份也夺走了！
t_en: ...No way! So in THEIR eyes I don't even count as a Masked Fool anymore? What a "wonderful surprise"! The impostor stole my identity too!

t_ch: 节哀顺变…
t_en: Sorry for your loss...

t_ch: 失去假面的愚者。
t_en: A Fool who has lost her Mask.

t_ch: 好消息是，至少我们中还有一个假面愚者。
t_en: The good news is, at least one of us is still a Masked Fool.

t_ch: 喂，这也太伤人了吧？！
t_en: Hey, that's way too harsh!

t_ch: 这就是你说的神迹？
t_en: So this is the miracle you meant?

t_ch: 是的。假面愚者间流传着一则神话——
t_en: Yes. Among the Masked Fools, a myth is told...

t_ch: 「当欢愉之主攀上存在之树的高枝，祂窥见真空冰冷可憎，星辰机械运转，万物意义让位于虚无。」
t_en: "When the Master of Elation climbed the highest boughs of the Tree of Existence, THEY glimpsed the cold, abhorrent void. The stars turned like machinery, and all meaning yielded to nihility."

t_ch: 「祂继续望去，直到目睹一名婴儿落地啼哭犹如受尽委屈，便忍不住纵声大笑，这清澈的笑声撕裂了冰冷死寂的宇宙，回荡诸界至今。」
t_en: "THEY gazed further, until THEY witnessed a newborn fall and cry as if wronged beyond measure. Then THEY burst out laughing. That crystalline laughter tore through the cold, dead universe, echoing through the realms to this day."

t_ch: 很多人都以为这撕裂宇宙的笑声只是某种比喻…但，很遗憾，它是真的。
t_en: Many think the laughter that tore apart the cosmos is just a metaphor... but sadly, it's real.

t_ch: 「酒馆」里的人称这东西为「太初笑声的回响」，它撕开了一个空间口子。
t_en: The tavern folks call this thing the Echo of Primordial Laughter. It tore open a spatial rift.

t_ch: 据说在其他「酒馆」分店里也有，不止一处，没人知道具体的数量，也没人知道它的存在到底意味着什么。我们对它大喊，它没有回音；往它里面扔些垃圾，它也统统笑纳……
t_en: They say other tavern branches have this too... in multiple locations. No one knows how many there are or what their existence means. When we shout into it, there's no echo. Toss in trash, and it merrily swallows it all.

t_ch: 没人知道这东西会不会继续开裂，把「酒馆」整个吞下…总之，它就在这儿，就在那儿，永不移动地对着我们笑。
t_en: No one knows if this thing will keep widening and swallow the tavern whole... Either way, it just sits there, right over there, motionless grinning at us forever.

t_ch: 该怎么得到乔瓦尼说的神启？
t_en: So how do we get this divine revelation that Giovanni mentioned?

t_ch: 它会告诉我们怎么做？
t_en: Will it tell us what to do?

t_ch: 把你的手伸进去就行。
t_en: Just stick your hand in there.

t_ch: 据说在面临命运转折的时刻，愚者只要把手伸进裂缝，就能摸到一件东西——「酒馆」里的人管这叫「乐子神的赐礼」。
t_en: It's said at a turning point of destiny, a Fool need only reach into the rift and grasp something. The tavern folks call it The Laughter's Gift.

t_ch: 谁都不知道这件东西会给祈求者带来什么…好运气还是坏运气？但有一点不需要怀疑，它一定会让你的命运变得更混沌。
t_en: No one knows what this thing will bring to those who seek it... fortune or otherwise? But one thing's for sure: your destiny's bound to get much more chaotic.

t_ch: 小灰毛，来试试看吧？
t_en: Hey, Gray Hair, wanna give it a try?

t_ch: 把手伸进其中…
t_en: Reach your hand in there...

t_ch: 等等，让我深呼吸一下。
t_en: Hold on, let me take a deep breath.

t_ch: 做好准备碰碰运气了吗？把手伸进裂隙，你就能摸到一件乐子神的赐礼。
t_en: Ready to try your luck? Reach into the fissure, and you'll find a gift from The Laughter.

t_ch: 把手伸进其中…
t_en: Reach your hand in there...

t_ch: 等等，让我深呼吸一下。
t_en: Hold on, let me take a deep breath.

t_ch: 你忐忑不安地将手伸进巨大的裂隙中——就把它想像成翁法罗斯的真言狮口好了，你不断自我催眠。
t_en: With trembling unease, you reach into the gaping fissure. Just pretend it's like the mouth of that Amphorean Verax Leo, you keep telling yourself.

t_ch: 阿基维利保佑…请看在阿基维利的面子上…请别为难我…我的手不好吃……
t_en: Akivili have mercy... in Akivili's name... please don't hurt me... My hand doesn't taste good...

t_ch: 你的手仿佛探入了一团柔软、漩动的流体里，一声怪异的猴子叫声在耳畔响起，让你想起了某个关于许愿的不祥故事，随后你察觉自己触到了什么。
t_en: Your hand seems to plunge into a soft, swirling fluid, and a bizarre monkey screech echoes in your ears, reminding you of an ominous story about wish-making. Then you sense that you've touched something.

t_ch: 一把抓住！
t_en: Got it!

t_ch: 摸索一下。
t_en: Let me feel around.

t_ch: 块状的棱角，硌手，微微刺痛，像是在地毯上踩中一小块玩具零件。
t_en: Angular and blocky, it hurts to hold, with a slight stinging sensation, like stepping on a small toy part hidden in the carpet.

t_ch: 这是……
t_en: Is this...

t_ch: 这是什么？
t_en: Huh. What's this?

t_ch: 你知道自己绝对见过这个东西，就在匹诺康尼。
t_en: You know for sure you've seen this thing before, right there in Penacony.

t_ch: 与「缭乱忍者」AKA乱破一同大战「御猿邪忍」的奇妙经历浮现心头。答案，如天际高悬的香蕉一般，让你忍不住道破了它的来历——
t_en: The extraordinary experience of fighting alongside "The Dazzling Ninja" AKA Rappa against the "Osaru Evil Ninja" flashed through your mind. The answer, like a banana hanging in the sky, compelled you to reveal its origin—

t_ch: 这蕉，我见过…
t_en: That banana... I've seen it before...

t_ch: 这是模因病毒蕉。
t_en: It's a memetic virus banana.

t_ch: 乐子神在上，祂真的回应了。但祂又塞给你另一种模因病毒是要干什么？
t_en: The Laughter above, THEY really answered! But what's THEIR deal giving you another memetic virus?

t_ch: 为了以毒攻毒蕉…
t_en: To fight virus with virus...

t_ch: 为了暂时免疫蕉…
t_en: To grant temporary immunity...

t_ch: 我明白了。
t_en: Ah, now I get it.

t_ch: 被「火花病毒」感染的观众当然没法反抗火花…我们闯进学校要不了多久就会变成那样——但如果在此之前，我们已经被另一种模因病毒感染了，那就很难说了。
t_en: Audiences infected with the Sparxie Virus naturally won't be able to resist Sparxie... We'd end up like that soon after breaking into the school. But if we're already infected with another memetic virus... Well, that changes things.

t_ch: 而这只「香蕉」的意义…正是给我们争取一点抵抗的时间。
t_en: And this banana serves exactly that purpose... to buy us some time to resist.

t_ch: 还真有「欢愉」独一份的幽默感啊，现在，我们走吧！
t_en: The Elation really has a twisted sense of humor, huh? All right, let's move!

t_ch: 你们俩…是谁？这儿一次只许一个愚者进来的规矩，你们是没听人讲过吗？
t_en: Who are you two? Haven't you heard the rule? Only one Fool's allowed in here at a time.

t_ch: 我是谁？一个路过的名侦探，还有他的助手。
t_en: Who am I? Just a passing detective, and his assistant.

t_ch: 是我睡太久了吗？还是这个世代的人类已经堕落到了变成猴子也满不在乎的地步了？
t_en: Have I been asleep too long? Or has humanity sunk so low they don't even care if they turn into monkeys?

t_ch: 与此同时，无知的罪犯们自以为发现了能改变他们命运的「宝藏」，但他们浑然不觉，自己手里握着的东西足以摧毁整个世界的理智。
t_en: Meanwhile, the ignorant criminals believed they had discovered a "treasure" that could change their destiny, completely unaware that what they held in their hands could shatter the sanity of the entire world.

t_ch: 老白，要战斗的时候就别念白了。
t_en: Hey, Mister N, cut the narration when it's time to fight.

t_ch: 那我总得做点什么吧？这样？
t_en: Well, I gotta do something, right? *shriek* Like this?

t_ch: 你开心就好。听好了，对面仨，把那只「香蕉」放在地上。现在离开，我可以既往不咎。
t_en: Whatever floats your boat. Now listen up, you three. Put that banana down and walk away, and I'll let bygones be bygones.

t_ch: 但你们要是敢动别的心思……
t_en: But if you try anything funny...

t_ch: 想动{F#她们}{M#他们}的话，得先趟过这些小玩意儿！
t_en: If you wanna get to them, you'll have to get through these little toys first!

t_ch: 咳咳，咳咳……
啧，这几个家伙真的是无名客？我活了大半辈子都没见过和模因病毒打交道的无名客。
t_en: Tch, are these folks really the Nameless? In all my years, I've never seen the Nameless dealing with memetic viruses.

t_ch: 如假包换，路过的无名客罢了。
t_en: The real deal. Just some Nameless passing through.

t_ch: 老白，我得追他们去了。你赶不上我的，回家等消息吧。
t_en: Mister N, I've got to go after them. You won't be able to keep up with me. Just head home and wait for the news.

t_ch: 我发誓，我不会让发生在你身上的悲剧在这个世界重演。
t_en: I swear I won't let the tragedy that happened to you repeat itself in this world.

t_ch: 这边！来这边！跑快些！你们该不会想被刚才那家伙逮住吧？
t_en: Over here! This way! Run faster! You don't wanna get caught by that guy back there, do you?

t_ch: 我说{F#姐们}{M#哥们}，你们这次落地到底惹到了多少棘手的人啊？
t_en: Yo {M#bro,}{F#sis,} just how many troublemakers did you people manage to tick off when you landed?

t_ch: 刚才那个又是谁啊？
t_en: So who was that just now?

t_ch: 我发誓，花火大人可绝对没招惹这样一号人物。
t_en: I swear, Miss Sparkle definitely hasn't gotten involved with someone like that.

t_ch: 你不是走了吗？
t_en: Didn't you leave already?

t_ch: 我今天眼皮直跳，知道事情多半要糟。老桑博对老顾客可是很念旧的，忍不住就回来看看你们，一看果然。
t_en: My eyelid's been twitching all day. Knew something was bound to go wrong. You know old Sampo's got a soft spot for his regulars, so I couldn't help but come check on you folks. And what do you know? I was right.

t_ch: 关键时刻，还得靠桑博…
t_en: When push comes to shove, Sampo's the one we can count on...

t_ch: 得亏老桑博服务到家，关键时刻救你们一命。
t_en: Good ol' Sampo came through with top-notch service, saving your lives in the nick of time.

t_ch: 谢谢你，桑博先生。
t_en: Thank you, Mr. Sampo.

t_ch: 既然我们已经做好了准备，也是时候该去绘世学院救人了。桑博先生，你知道酒馆里有哪道门可以尽快回到城市里？
t_en: Now that we're all set, it's time to head to Graphia Academy for the rescue mission. Mr. Sampo, do you know which door in the tavern can get us back to the city the quickest?

t_ch: 哈哈，这还用问，要说对酒馆各种「暗道」「门径」的熟悉，你还真找不到第二个能和老桑博比的——
t_en: Haha, you don't even need to ask! When it comes to knowing all the "secret passages" and "shortcuts" in this tavern, you won't find anyone who can match old Sampo...

t_ch: 那就快带路吧！
t_en: Then lead the way!

t_ch: 走吧，跟紧我。
t_en: Let's move. Stay close.

t_ch: 你、你要做什么？
t_en: Wh—What are you going to do?

t_ch: 「打」开你啊。
t_en: I'm gonna "break" you open.

t_ch: 看得出来，{NICKNAME}，「酒馆」没早一点向你发出邀请，是他们没眼光。
t_en: I see, {NICKNAME}... the tavern not inviting you sooner was their loss.

t_ch: 往里面走吧。
t_en: Come on in.

t_ch: 这些酒桶里装的饮料，真是笑话酿成的吗？
t_en: Is the drink in these barrels really brewed from jokes?

t_ch: 没错。大姐头你懂的不少嘛？
t_en: Yep. You know your stuff, ma'am!

t_ch: 听过一些传闻罢了。
t_en: Just heard some rumors, that's all.

t_ch: 让我教教两位怎么开路。
t_en: Let me show you how to open a path.

t_ch: 按着乐谱演奏，道路自然就会敞开。
t_en: Play according to the sheet music, and the path will naturally open up.

t_ch: 钢架居然展开了。
t_en: The steel frame actually opened.

t_ch: 好多…洗手台？
t_en: So many... sinks?

t_ch: 你带我们来这儿干嘛？
t_en: Why bring us here?

t_ch: 桑博，这玩意儿真的有用吗？
t_en: Sampo, does this thing even work?

t_ch: 包有用的，你还信不过我老桑博吗？
t_en: Of course it works! Don't you trust your old pal Sampo?

t_ch: 到底是哪个除灵师这么不负责任啊？
t_en: Which irresponsible exorcist did this?

t_ch: 酒馆的下水道系统不是垃圾站！
t_en: The tavern's sewer system is not a dump!

t_ch: 哎呀，前几天我遇到一个粉丝…
t_en: Oh man, the other day I ran into this fan...

t_ch: 非要给我看他在网上新发现的「地狱笑话」，说特别好笑。
t_en: They insisted on showing me some "gallows humor" jokes they'd found online, saying they were absolutely hilarious.

t_ch: 但他只是给我看了某人被反物质军团杀害的视频，却自顾自大笑了起来。
t_en: But all he did was show me a video of someone being killed by the Antimatter Legion, then burst out laughing.

t_ch: 你们也一起来品品这个「地狱笑话」吧！
t_en: Come, let's all enjoy this "gallows humor" together!

t_ch: 接下来去哪？
t_en: So, where are we heading next?

t_ch: …去「致命玩笑」看看。
t_en: ...Let's check out "Fatal Joke."

t_ch: 致命玩笑？那又是什么地方？
t_en: Fatal Joke? What kind of place is that?

t_ch: 可能…是个「医院」吧。
t_en: Maybe... some kind of "hospital."

t_ch: 这里是…二维市？怎么可能？
t_en: Is this... Duomension City? How is that possible?

t_ch: 「酒馆」的空间结构本来就离奇，能看到二维市倒也不奇怪。
t_en: The tavern's spatial structure is bizarre to begin with. It's not that strange to see Duomension City here.

t_ch: 小灰毛，我们走吧，我不想和这个庸医浪费时间了。
t_en: Gray Hair, let's go. I don't want to waste any more time with this quack.

t_ch: 花火小姐，在诊断结束前，还请您在这个房间稍等片刻。
t_en: Miss Sparkle, please wait in this room until the consultation is complete.

t_ch: 诊断结果出来了，花火小姐！
t_en: The diagnosis results are in, Miss Sparkle!

t_ch: 喂！什么情况！怎么就把我摁在这儿了！
t_en: Hey! What's going on! Why am I pinned here!?

t_ch: 小灰毛？大姐头？来帮帮我？
t_en: Gray Hair? Ma'am Navigator? A little help?

t_ch: 你们需要我，我可以帮助你们打败火花！
t_en: You need me! I can help you defeat Sparxie!

t_ch: Bravo！
t_en: Bravo!

t_ch: 太好笑了！
t_en: That's hilarious!

t_ch: 接下来该进战斗桥段了吧？
t_en: So, is it time for the combat sequence now?

t_ch: 呜呼，花火大人劫后余生！
t_en: Phew! Miss Sparkle lives to fight another day!

t_ch: 这是什么？
t_en: Huh. What's this?

t_ch: 好大的星琼！
t_en: Whoa, that's one massive Stellar Jade!

t_ch: 走吧，进去瞧瞧。
t_en: Let's go in and take a look.

t_ch: 老乔，这是那个冒牌货留下来的东西吗？
t_en: Gio, did the impostor leave this behind?

t_ch: 我怎么知道。清点工作做到现在，我才发现有这号货不对板的玩意儿。
t_en: How should I know? I've been doing inventory all this time. I only just now noticed something fishy about this thing.

t_ch: 这颗石头…很危险。
t_en: This rock... is dangerous.

t_ch: …完了，全完了！
t_en: ...We're done for, game over!

t_ch: 我的「反花火武装」变成了一只破烂孔雀……
t_en: My Anti-Sparkle Weapon has turned into a failed science project...

t_ch: 这似乎不是咱们进来的地方了……
t_en: This doesn't look like where we came in...

t_ch: 别担心，我让老乔开了条近路。
t_en: Don't worry. I had old Gio open a shortcut.

t_ch: 虽然很不甘心…但是，小灰毛，它向你敞开了，灯球承认了你。
t_en: I hate to admit it... but Gray Hair, it's opened for you. The light orb has acknowledged you.

t_ch: 舞池下面居然有这么多人……
t_en: Wait... there are that many people under the dance floor...?

t_ch: 这儿的尘灵居然会随灯光变色。
t_en: The wispae here actually change color with the lights.

t_ch: 这就是酒馆的日常，很嗨吧~
t_en: Just another night at the tavern... Fun, right?

t_ch: 真想在列车上也装个灯球啊。
t_en: Man, I really wish we had one of these light orbs on the Express.

t_ch: 这是什么？马桶搋子？
t_en: What's this? A plunger?

t_ch: 比起酒馆，这里更像个迷宫。
t_en: This place feels more like a maze than a tavern.

t_ch: 这本就是个时空迷宫啊！
t_en: Well, it IS a space-time maze after all!

t_ch: 这酒馆可真够大的啊。
t_en: This tavern is huge!

t_ch: 还好吧，我还嫌这儿好玩的地方不够多呢。
t_en: Eh, it's alright. I just wish there were more fun spots around here.

t_ch: 危险？看我一棒给它敲碎！
t_en: Dangerous? I'll smash it with one swing!

t_ch: 没错，敲碎了全部带回去。
t_en: You got it. Smash every last one and bring 'em all back.

t_ch: 后面有东西…那是钥匙吗？
t_en: Something's back there... Is that a key?

t_ch: 钥匙融进了水里…水面要有变化了。
t_en: The key has dissolved into the water... the water's surface is about to change.

t_ch: 这里可是共愿帮的地盘。
t_en: This is Synwish Syndicate's turf.

t_ch: 还有不少人敢往这来呢！
t_en: Can't believe people still have the guts to come here!

t_ch: 总有人来这里找「酒馆」…
t_en: People always come here looking for a "tavern"...

t_ch: 但还没人找到过呢。
t_en: But no one's ever found it.

t_ch: 鸽川里面不让游泳！
t_en: No swimming in Dovebrook!

t_ch: 这就是仙舟第一杀手？
t_en: This is the Xianzhou's Number One Assassin?

t_ch: 恐怖如斯、恐怖如斯…
t_en: So terrifying, so terrifying...

t_ch: 怎么有个人不像仙舟人？
t_en: How come there's someone who doesn't look like they're from the Xianzhou?

t_ch: 嘘，别瞎问。不要命了？
t_en: Shh, don't ask questions. You got a death wish or something?

t_ch: 他那长枪看着真凶啊。
t_en: That spear of his looks seriously deadly.

t_ch: 别和他对上视线…
t_en: Don't make eye contact with him...

t_ch: 小心捅你一万个透明窟窿！
t_en: It'll poke a thousand holes right through you!

t_ch: 都给我精神点！别跌份！
t_en: Everyone, look sharp! Don't let me down!

t_ch: 蹒跚的愚者静静地躺在地上。和之前比起来，他变得又小又薄，适合随身携带。
t_en: The staggering Fool lies quietly on the ground. Compared to before, he's become small and thin, a size perfect for carrying around.

t_ch: 但你非要游，我也没办法。
t_en: But if you insist on taking a dip, I can't stop you.

t_ch: 我没醉，嘿嘿，我没醉！
t_en: I'm not drunk, hehe, I'm not drunk!

t_ch: 鸽川的水，我的泪～
t_en: Dovebrook's waters are my tears~

t_ch: 花火被一股无形的力量束缚在了这张床上，这股难以违抗的力量你在早上赖床时曾有体会——没有人能战胜它！
t_en: Sparkle finds herself bound to the bed by an invisible force. This irresistible power you've experienced yourself on mornings when you just can't get up. No one can defeat it!

t_ch: 花火被固定在了这张床上！不知钟珊用了什么法子：神话里看不见的绳索？无形的大手？念动力？
t_en: Sparkle was pinned to the bed! Who knows what trick Zhongshan pulled: invisible ropes from myths? An unseen giant hand? Telekinesis?

t_ch: 「共愿帮征用中」…看来得找别的路去对岸了。
t_en: "Under Synwish Syndicate requisition"... Looks like we'll have to find another way across.

t_ch: 往前，追上这个「月亮的倒影」。
t_en: Keep going, chase that "moon's reflection."

t_ch: 「月亮的倒影」逃跑了，追上它。
t_en: The "moon's reflection" is getting away! Go after it!

t_ch: 睁大眼睛看好了…
t_en: Keep your eyes peeled...

t_ch: 切不可放松警惕…
t_en: Don't let your guard down...

t_ch: 以「智识」之名，横渡银河——
t_en: In the name of Erudition, traverse the cosmos.

t_ch: 我们再度重逢，天外的救世主。
t_en: We meet again, Deliverer from beyond the sky.

t_ch: 什么鬼？！
t_en: What the heck!?

t_ch: 我知道，我们还会相见…
t_en: Just as I expected...

t_ch: 我并非您熟知的神礼观众，而是假面愚者，赞达尔•贰•桑原。
t_en: I am not the Theoros you know, but the Masked Fool, Zandar Two Kuwabara.

t_ch: 这一次，我选择走上「欢愉」，以我的幽默感击破博识尊。
t_en: This time, I choose to walk the path of Elation, and I shall defeat Nous with my sense of humor.

t_ch: 给我气笑了。
t_en: That's somehow both hilarious and infuriating.

t_ch: 我好像猜到你是谁了…
t_en: I think I've figured out who you are...

t_ch: 呵，毕竟我早就说过……
t_en: Hah, after all, I did say before...

t_ch: 最近的梦想，是加入天才俱乐部，扮演一个天才！
t_en: My latest dream is to join the Genius Society and play the role of a genius!

t_ch: 小灰毛，三月宝宝，你们好啊。
t_en: Hey there, Gray Hair and darling March!

t_ch: 花火？！可恶，原来是你……
t_en: Sparkle!? Ugh, it was you all along...

t_ch: 是不是很意外？不过这次，我是作为「二相乐园」的文化顾问，特地来为列车当导游的。
t_en: Surprised? Well, this time I'm here as Planarcadia's cultural advisor, specially appointed to be the tour director for the Express crew.

t_ch: 你把真正的导游怎么了？
t_en: What did you do to the real tour director?

t_ch: 什么话，我就是你们的导游啊。花导。
t_en: What are you talking about? I am your director. Director Sparkle.

t_ch: 公司会雇佣假面愚者？
t_en: The IPC hires Masked Fools?

t_ch: 什么话，这里是「欢愉」的世界，愚者遍地都是。做生意绕开我们？想都别想。
t_en: What are you talking about? This is the world of Elation. Fools are everywhere. Don't even think about doing business here without us.

t_ch: 你是幕后黑手吧。
t_en: You're the mastermind behind everything, aren't you?

t_ch: 你猜对啦，花火大人正盘算着将两位的命运玩弄于股掌间呢，怕了吗？
t_en: Bingo! Miss Sparkle is planning to toy with both of your destinies as if they're just her playthings. Scared yet?

t_ch: 所以，花导是导游的导？
t_en: Wait, you're a tour director?

t_ch: 回答正确！星穹列车加十分。
t_en: Correct! Ten points for the Astral Express!

t_ch: 我知道，在匹诺康尼，我们是有些小小的纠葛，但你们不是玩得很开心嘛？
t_en: I know we had some minor entanglements in Penacony, but you all had fun there, didn't you?

t_ch: 现在，星穹列车成大英雄了。那位{RUBY_B#真珠}首席执行官{RUBY_E#}希望找一位友好相处的熟人带你们参观这里。我就接下这差事咯。
t_en: Now, the Astral Express has become quite the hero. Her Excellency the CEO wanted to find someone who got along with you to show you around this place. So I took on that job!

t_ch: 她到底对「友好相处」这个词有什么误解啊……
t_en: What kind of misunderstanding does she have about the phrase "got along"...

t_ch: 时间滴答滴答流逝——幸好这里没有炸弹！所以，你们到底跟不跟我走啊？
t_en: Time's ticking away! Thankfully there's no bomb here! So, are you coming with me or what?

t_ch: 走吧，这也是开拓的一环。
t_en: Let's go, this is also part of Trailblazing.

t_ch: 小心，你面前可是两大泰坦。
t_en: Watch out, you've got two Titans right in front of you.

t_ch: 行吧。但接下来，可别再扮成别人的样子啦！
t_en: Fine. But from now on, no more pretending to be someone else!

t_ch: 真没劲。那…出发吧？
t_en: Lame. Anyway... shall we get going?

t_ch: 这是…列车吗？好帅气啊！
t_en: Is this... a train? Looking sharp!

t_ch: 多谢夸奖！
t_en: Thanks for the compliment!

t_ch: 列车开口说话了！
t_en: The train just spoke!

t_ch: 眼前这一位，既是列车，也是妖怪。
t_en: Before us stands not just a train, but a strangeling.

t_ch: 妖怪这种称呼，早就过时啦！
t_en: The term strangeling is so outdated!

t_ch: 时代变了，客人们。请按《灵物保护法》的说法称呼我们：幻造种。
t_en: Times have changed, dear guests. Please refer to the Faekind Protection Act. We prefer to be called imagenae.

t_ch: 一板一眼的，这么喜欢公司教科书里的学名？算了，就依你吧……
t_en: Ugh, so by-the-book. You really love those fancy scientific names from the IPC textbooks, huh? Fine, have it your way...

t_ch: 这位是「二相乐园」的特产物种：<color=#dbc291ff>由画中诞生的灵物，「幻造种」</color>。
t_en: This here is a species unique to Planarcadia: <color=#dbc291ff>an imagenae, or a being born from paintings</color>.

t_ch: 从画中诞生？
t_en: Born from paintings?

t_ch: …二次元生物？
t_en: ...An interplanar creature?

t_ch: 很聪明嘛。一下子就理解了。
t_en: Pretty sharp, aren't you? You caught on quick.

t_ch: 很久很久以前，这里的画家们发现，只要勾画出想象中的生灵，赋予它们名字，就能将造物带入现实。
t_en: Once upon a time, the artists here discovered that by sketching creatures from their imagination and giving them names, they could bring their creations into reality.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>这种相信未有之物的信心叫「愿力」</color>，它维持着幻造种的存在。要我说，这股力量一定是乐子神的恩赐！
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>This belief in things yet to exist is called "Wishpower,"</color> and it sustains the existence of imagenae. If you ask me, this power must be a blessing from The Laughter!

t_ch: 哎呀扯远啦！这位是「疾锋龙列车」，负责交通运输的幻造种，跟你们也算是同行。
t_en: Oops, got sidetracked! This here is the "Bullet Wyrm Express," an imagenae in charge of transportation. You could say they're in the same line of work as you folks.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，两位是星穹列车的无名客吧？你们的八卦，我肚子里的乘客们常常念叨呢！
t_en: Hahaha, so you two must be the Nameless from the Astral Express? The passengers in my stomach are always gossiping about you!

t_ch: 有机会，乘坐我试试吧～看看星穹列车和我比起来，到底谁更快！哈哈哈哈哈！
t_en: When you get a chance, hop aboard and give me a try~ Let's see who's faster, me or the Astral Express! Hahahahaha!

t_ch: 谢啦，有空咱一定！不过，这里的列车居然是活的生命，幻造种…真是不可思议。
t_en: Thanks! We'll definitely do that when we get the chance! But man, who would've thought that the Express here is actually a living being, an imagenae... That's absolutely mind-blowing.

t_ch: 接下来，花导还要带你去见更多不可思议的东西，可别晕咯！
t_en: Next up, I'm gonna show you even more mind-blowing stuff, so try not to faint!

t_ch: 快速、安全、舒适！有机会请乘坐我试试吧～
t_en: Fast, safe, and comfy! Come take a ride with me some time~

t_ch: 好家伙，站台上有警察？花火，你该不会要被当场逮捕了吧？
t_en: Holy smokes, there are cops at the station? Sparkle, don't tell me you're about to get arrested on the spot?

t_ch: 几位好，欢迎来到<color=#dbc291ff>二维市</color>！
t_en: Hello everyone, welcome to <color=#dbc291ff>Duomension City</color>!

t_ch: 得知星穹列车到访，不少市民起了个大早，专程来一睹无名客的风采。要是有人做出出格举动，请随时呼叫我们。
t_en: Having heard about the Astral Express's visit, many citizens got up early just to catch a glimpse of the Nameless. If anyone acts out of line, please don't hesitate to call us.

t_ch: 嗨呀，小灰毛，三月宝宝，你们可是大明星啦！
t_en: Ooh, Gray Hair and darling March, you're real celebrities now!

t_ch: 啊啊啊啊快看！
t_en: Aaaah look!

t_ch: 那粉毛女孩！有点小兔子可可爱爱的感觉，她就是「三月兔」的原型？
t_en: That pink-haired girl! She's got this adorable little bunny vibe going on. Is she the inspiration for "March Bunny"?

t_ch: 她边上的灰头发{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}也太帅气了！应该就是「球棒浣熊」吧！
t_en: And that {M#guy}{F#girl} with gray hair next to her is so dashing! {M#He}{F#She} must be the "Baseball Raccoon"!

t_ch: 可{F#她}{M#他}一点也不像浣熊啊？
t_en: But {F#s}he doesn't look anything like a raccoon...

t_ch: 二相乐园的朋友们！你们好！
t_en: Friends from Planarcadia! Hello!

t_ch: 二相乐园的朋友们，你们好啊！
t_en: Hey there, friends from Planarcadia!

t_ch: 虽然不知道发生了什么，但是…你们好！
t_en: I don't know what's going on, but... hello there!

t_ch: 可恶！我也可可爱爱的啊？
t_en: Come on! I'm adorable too!

t_ch: 喂，你！对，就是你！快夸我可爱！
t_en: Hey, you! Yes, you! Hurry up and say I'm cute!

t_ch: 叫错名字是天大的错误！
t_en: Getting my name wrong is basically a crime!

t_ch: 给我记好了，是「银河球棒侠」，不是什么「球棒浣熊」！
t_en: Drill this into your head. It's the "Galactic Baseballer," not some "Baseball Raccoon"!

t_ch: 「三月兔」又是怎么回事啊？
t_en: And what's this "March Bunny" thing about?

t_ch: {F#她}{M#他}朝我们说话了！乐子神在上！这是我人生中最精彩的时刻！
t_en: {M#He}{F#She} spoke to us! By The Laughter! This is the most amazing moment of my life!

t_ch: 看这边——看这边——列车组——我们——请给我签名——三月小姐我爱你——浣熊{F#小姐}{M#先生}给我一个吻吧——
t_en: Here... Look here... Express Crew... We... Please give me your autograph... Miss March I love you... {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} Raccoon, give me a kiss...

t_ch: 疯狂签名！
t_en: Giving autographs like crazy!

t_ch: 我拿到了——浣熊大人的签名！——我也要我也要——
t_en: I got it... The autograph of the Great Raccoon! I want it too...

t_ch: 向粉丝频频鞠躬！
t_en: Bow repeatedly to the fans!

t_ch: 啊啊啊啊——
t_en: Aaaah!

t_ch: 对大家抛出飞吻！
t_en: Blow kisses to everyone!

t_ch: 啊啊啊啊——
t_en: Aaaah!

t_ch: 请各位保持秩序！
t_en: Everyone, please maintain order!

t_ch: 这里的人，实在太热情了…我还是第一次遇到这种阵仗。这就是「狂热粉丝」吗？
t_en: The people here are way too passionate... I've never experienced anything like this before. So this is what having "rabid fans" feels like?

t_ch: 被众星捧月的感觉，是不是很让人陶醉？可别陷在里面，出不来哟？
t_en: Feels intoxicating to be the center of attention, doesn't it? Just don't get too caught up in it. You might not find your way out!

t_ch: 是不错！
t_en: Yeah, it's amazing!

t_ch: 可人也太多了吧？
t_en: But isn't it a bit too crowded?

t_ch: 两位老板，不要沉迷和粉丝互动啦。今天的行程安排可是很紧张的。
t_en: Excuse me. Bosses, stop getting caught up in fan interactions! Today's schedule is really packed.

t_ch: 我头上这根猫条先生！能告诉我怎么带两位离开这儿吗？
t_en: Hey, you up there, Mr. Noodle-Looking-Cat! Could you tell me how to get these two out of here?

t_ch: 这位客人，说话好伤人！我是猫条站长，负责泊地站的工作！
t_en: Excuse me, that's quite hurtful! I am Stationmaster Meowton, responsible for overseeing Perdido Station!

t_ch: 猫咪…果然是液体的，等等，这只猫咪…是车站的站长？这个世界，也太怪了吧！
t_en: Whoa... Cats really are liquid! Wait a sec, this kitty is the stationmaster? This world is so weird!

t_ch: 车站已经被包围了，要是哪位贵客今天在这被狂热粉丝擦破点皮，薅走几根头发，我那点可怜的退休金就全泡汤了！
t_en: The station's completely surrounded! If any of our esteemed guests gets so much as a scratch or loses a few strands of hair to these rabid fans today, my measly pension will go right down the drain!

t_ch: 所以，我为星穹列车的贵宾准备了「员工通道」，请快快从那边离开车站！
t_en: So I've prepared the "staff passage" for our Astral Express VIPs. Please hurry and exit the station that way!

t_ch: 拜托，请不要往那边走，会引发混乱的！
t_en: Please don't go that way. It'll cause chaos!

t_ch: 员工通道…？
t_en: Staff passage...?

t_ch: 准确地说，是幻造种的员工通道。很久以前，整~个~乐园都被收进过一幅「画卷」里喔。
t_en: To be precise, it's the imagenae staff passage. A long time ago, ALL of this arcadia was sealed inside a "scroll painting."

t_ch: 那样还能有人活着？
t_en: And people survived that?

t_ch: 对画中人来说，不过是进入了另一个维度，很安全的。
t_en: For people inside the painting, they just entered another dimension. It was completely safe.

t_ch: 二相乐园也不平面啊？
t_en: Planarcadia isn't a flat plane though.

t_ch: 那是因为有人将他们带回了现实。猜猜为什么公司会在这儿？
t_en: That's because someone brought everything back to reality. Care to guess why the IPC is here?

t_ch: 这座一度沉入画中的国度，就是被公司打捞回来的。
t_en: This nation that was once lost in a painting was salvaged by the IPC.

t_ch: 这是通往画中的通道？
t_en: Is this the passage into the painting?

t_ch: 没错，「二相乐园」不得不品的特色，被称为<color=#dbc291ff>「二次元JUMP！」</color>的跃迁入口。
t_en: That's right. It's Planarcadia's must-see attraction: the portal entrance known as the <color=#dbc291ff>"Interplanar Jump!"</color>.

t_ch: 虽然文明回到了画外，但画中的世界没有被遗弃，反而成为了人和妖怪生活的一部分。
t_en: Though civilization has moved beyond the canvas, the world within the painting wasn't abandoned. Instead, it became part of life for both humans and strangelings.

t_ch: 来吧两位，跟上我的脚步，一起跳进——
t_en: Come on, you two. Follow my lead and let's dive into the...

t_ch: <size=36>二！次！元！</size>
t_en: <size=36>Inter! Planar!</size>

t_ch: JUMP！
t_en: JUMP!

t_ch: 让我再准备一下。
t_en: I need a moment to get ready.

t_ch: 小灰毛，你怕啦？咕咚一下钻进去就好。
t_en: Gray Hair, getting cold feet? Just dive right in!

t_ch: 来吧两位，跟上我的脚步，一起跳进二次元！
t_en: Come on, you two. Follow my lead and let's dive into the Interplanar...

t_ch: JUMP！
t_en: JUMP!

t_ch: 让我再准备一下。
t_en: I need a moment to get ready.

t_ch: 小灰毛，你怕啦？咕咚一下钻进去就好。
t_en: Gray Hair, getting cold feet? Just dive right in!

t_ch: 我…我差点以为…自己要上天堂了。
t_en: I... I thought... I was about to go to heaven.

t_ch: 每次旅行都要从高处掉下来…
t_en: Every trip involves falling from high places...

t_ch: 小场面，我都习惯了。
t_en: No big deal. I'm already used to this.

t_ch: 周围奇奇怪怪的东西还挺多。
t_en: There's quite a lot of weird stuff around here.

t_ch: 这些…全都是幻造种吗？
t_en: Are all these... imagenae?

t_ch: 没错，这就是「二相乐园」，妖怪纵情往来的浮世，欢愉妙想汇成的乐土。
t_en: That's right. This is Planarcadia, a world where strangelings roam freely and a paradise woven from elation and wild imagination.

t_ch: 那趴在高塔上的大怪兽，正盯着咱们瞧呢…心里有点发毛。
t_en: That huge creature perched on the tower is staring right at us... It's giving me the creeps.

t_ch: 他倒是不轻易吃人，除非看到——小粉毛！
t_en: It doesn't usually eat people, unless it spots... pink hair!

t_ch: 唉唉啊啊啊！干什么，你又一惊一乍吓唬人！
t_en: Waaaaah! What's the matter with you, jumping around and scaring people!?

t_ch: 这大家伙叫「雷虬」。别担心，在他眼里，咱们只是地上的小蚂蚁，还不够塞牙缝呢。
t_en: That big fella is called a "thundercoil." Don't worry, to him, we're just tiny ants on the ground, not even enough to get stuck between his teeth.

t_ch: 雷虬…听这名字，他不该飞在天上打雷下雨吗？怎么盘在柱子上一动不动？
t_en: Thundercoil... With a name like that, shouldn't he be flying around making thunder and rain? Why is he just wrapped around that pillar without moving an inch?

t_ch: 时代变啦。公司颁布了法令，要求幻造种融入「文明社会」。
t_en: Times have changed. The IPC issued a decree requiring imagenae to integrate into "civilized society."

t_ch: 在法律上，他们拥有和人类等同的权利，这意味着——他们也得工作！
t_en: Legally, they have the same rights as humans, which means... they have to work too!

t_ch: 什么？妖怪也要工作？
t_en: What? Strangelings have to work too?

t_ch: 这就是公司经营的世界啊，谁敢剥夺幻造种工作的权利？
t_en: That's life under IPC management. Who'd dare deny an imagenae their right to work?

t_ch: 现在，雷虬为电视塔增幅电波信号，让全球用户都能稳定收看电视台放送的节目：无聊的直播，无聊的整人游戏……
t_en: Now, the thundercoil amplifies TV tower signals, ensuring users worldwide can watch the station's broadcasts without interruption: boring livestreams, lame prank shows...

t_ch: 啊，还有列车到来的大新闻——
t_en: Oh, and there's the big news about the Express arriving...

t_ch: 快看，{NICKNAME}，那广告牌又是怎么回事？！
t_en: Look, {NICKNAME}, what's up with that billboard!?

t_ch: 这是…我？我这么有名吗！
t_en: Is that... me? Am I really that famous!?

t_ch: 不公平！怎么只有{F#她}{M#他}的巨幅海报，我的呢？
t_en: That's not fair! Why does {F#s}he get a huge poster while I don't?

t_ch: 这人长得和我真像…
t_en: That person looks just like me...

t_ch: 哪里是「像」啊！这不就是你吗！
t_en: What do you mean by "looks like"? That IS you!

t_ch: 侵犯我的肖像权了吧？
t_en: That's gotta be a violation of my publicity rights!

t_ch: 要是挂在这儿的是本姑娘，我可不会说出这么煞风景的话。
t_en: If it were me up there, I wouldn't be saying such negative stuff.

t_ch: 呵，公司为了捧你们，真是造足了势头啊。
t_en: Hah, the IPC's really pulling out all the stops to hype you guys up.

t_ch: 可别怪花导多嘴，公司人花出去的每一分钱，都是为了几百几千倍的回报，你们可千万要当心哦。
t_en: Don't mind me saying this, but for every penny the IPC spends on you, they expect a return in the hundreds or thousands. You'd better watch your back.

t_ch: 咱们好不容易才从人堆里钻出来，怎么又要钻回去？
t_en: We just managed to squeeze our way out of that crowd. Why do you want to dive back in?

t_ch: 这、这是什么？…飞头智械吗？
t_en: Wh—What is that...? A decapitated Intellitron?

t_ch: 这是前一次「幻月游戏」结束后诞生的新生命。幻造种「机铠」，由公司一手打造。
t_en: This is a new life-form that was born after the last Phantasmoon Games. An imagenae called a "mechatron," created by the IPC.

t_ch: 这也是画出来的？
t_en: So this was drawn too?

t_ch: 严格来说，是由公司机器绘制的。
t_en: Technically speaking, it was drawn by IPC machines.

t_ch: 虽说在二相乐园，创生幻造种的工作一直是由绘师负责…但公司嘛，总想着用他们那套工业化思维改进一切。
t_en: Even though in Planarcadia, the creation of imagenae has always been the artists' responsibility... for the IPC, well, they always want to improve everything with their industrial mindset.

t_ch: 这也是生命？
t_en: This counts as a lifeform?

t_ch: 这东西也是幻造种？看起来和机甲没什么差别嘛？
t_en: This thing is also an imagenae? Doesn't look that different from a mech to me.

t_ch: 年轻的女孩，我必须纠正你的认知偏差。我们和机甲绝不是同类。
t_en: Young lady, I must correct your cognitive bias. We are absolutely not the same as mechs.

t_ch: 啊，机铠说话了！
t_en: Ah, the mechatron spoke!

t_ch: 我们的诞生不像机甲那样繁琐，诞生的我们也不像智械那么脆弱。因为我们不需要安装智械的逻辑中枢，也无需置入机甲的能源动力炉。
t_en: Our creation isn't as complicated as mechs, and we're not as fragile as Intellitrons when we're born. That's because we don't need to install an Intellitron's logic center, nor do we require a mech's power reactor.

t_ch: 向模具流道注入材料，压铸零件，组合拼装。我们机铠一族就此完成，能飞、能跑、能跳，绝不逊色任何科技产物。
t_en: Pour materials into the mold channels, die-cast the parts, assemble them together. And just like that, we mechatron are complete. We can fly, run, and jump, second to none among technological marvels.

t_ch: 我明白，靠愿力就能动起来是吧？
t_en: I get it. So you move with Wishpower, I suppose?

t_ch: 你很懂嘛！
t_en: Seems someone knows their stuff!

t_ch: 你说上一次「幻月游戏」？
t_en: You mentioned the last Phantasmoon Games?

t_ch: 是啊，十五年前的事了。满月期间，是愿力最高涨的时刻，幻造种也会借这个机会繁衍兴盛……
t_en: Yeah, that was fifteen years ago. Wishpower reaches its peak during the full moon, and so imagenae take this chance to multiply and thrive...

t_ch: 就跟纪录片里面说的那样：春天到了，万物复苏，又到了妖怪们繁衍的时节。
t_en: Just like they say in documentaries: When spring arrives and nature awakens, it's mating season for the strangelings again.

t_ch: 新鲜劲也该过去了吧？咱们继续走着。
t_en: The novelty's probably worn off by now. Let's keep moving.

t_ch: 花导，这些人排个长龙是在干什么？
t_en: Director Sparkle, what's with this huge line of people?
